title: Prevent Jump Statements in 'finally' Blocks
description: Jump statements such as return, break, throw etc., when used in a 'finally' block, can suppress the propagation of any unhandled Throwable which was thrown in the 'try' or 'catch' block. This rule alerts when a jump statement would force control flow to leave a 'finally' block.
recommendation: Avoid using jump statements in 'finally' blocks. These can prevent exceptions thrown in 'try' or 'catch' blocks from being propagated.
impact: Using jump statements in 'finally' blocks can lead to suppressed errors and unexpected behavior in the application. This can make debugging difficult and can potentially lead to unnoticed bugs in the application.

checks.message: Avoid using jump statements in 'finally' blocks.
